$meta.step: Step 2 — Design System and UX Foundation
$meta.description: Iconography policy and the semantic icon mapping. No icon binary is committed.

icon.strokeWidth.description: Stroke width for the outline icon style at size.icon.lg.

icon.style.description: Canonical icon style: geometric outline, 2px stroke, rounded terminals. Filled variants are reserved for selected navigation states.

icon.source.description: Icons are drawn from a permissively licensed open-source set, referenced by name in the component specifications. No icon binary or font is committed to this repository in Step 2. The specific set is selected in Step 3 and is PLANNED, NOT IMPLEMENTED.

icon.meaningRule.description: An icon never carries meaning alone. Every meaning-bearing icon is paired with a text label or an accessible name.
icon.meaningRule.accessibilityNotes: A decorative icon is hidden from assistive technology. A meaning-bearing icon exposes an accessible name in Bahasa Indonesia.
prohibitedUsage: Never the sole indicator of status.
Never the sole label of an action in a destructive confirmation.

icon.semantic.success.description: Canonical icon name for the success semantic. Always paired with text and with color.semantic.success where a matching colour token exists — three redundant signals, so status never depends on colour alone.

icon.semantic.warning.description: Canonical icon name for the warning semantic. Always paired with text and with color.semantic.warning where a matching colour token exists — three redundant signals, so status never depends on colour alone.
